verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Almagreira After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 48 kJ, 10 s period, lully 15/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully.
best.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a small window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 37 kJ, 10 s period, lully 12/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; 10-13s at low tide is a bit sucky and technical on the reef shelves.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide helps a little, but it is not a perfect match.
warnings: 10-13s at low tide is a bit sucky and technical on the reef shelves

photo.alt: Photo of Cantinho da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 127 kJ, 10 s period, consistent 74/100, and wind is the main risk.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches.  Watch: spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 10-13s at high tide can get fat and slow in the sheltered bay; Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide helps a bit, but the bay still needs enough push.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
10-13s at high tide can get fat and slow in the sheltered bay
Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size

photo.alt: Photo of Meio da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 39 kJ, 10 s period, lully 23/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Meio da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size; Meio da Baia may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
